speaker: BOY
text: Good grandam, tell us, is our father dead?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: No, boy.

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: Why do you weep so oft, and beat your breast,

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: And cry “O Clarence, my unhappy son”?

speaker: BOY
text: Why do you look on us and shake your head,

speaker: BOY
text: And call us orphans, wretches, castaways,

speaker: BOY
text: If that our noble father were alive?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: My pretty cousins, you mistake me both.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I do lament the sickness of the King,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: As loath to lose him, not your father’s death.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: It were lost sorrow to wail one that’s lost.

speaker: BOY
text: Then, you conclude, my grandam, he is dead.

speaker: BOY
text: The King mine uncle is to blame for it.

speaker: BOY
text: God will revenge it, whom I will importune

speaker: BOY
text: With earnest prayers, all to that effect.

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: And so will I.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Peace, children, peace. The King doth love you well.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Incapable and shallow innocents,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: You cannot guess who caused your father’s death.

speaker: BOY
text: Grandam, we can, for my good uncle Gloucester

speaker: BOY
text: Told me the King, provoked to it by the Queen,

speaker: BOY
text: Devised impeachments to imprison him;

speaker: BOY
text: And when my uncle told me so, he wept,

speaker: BOY
text: And pitied me, and kindly kissed my cheek,

speaker: BOY
text: Bade me rely on him as on my father,

speaker: BOY
text: And he would love me dearly as a child.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, that deceit should steal such gentle shape,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And with a virtuous visor hide deep vice.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: He is my son, ay, and therein my shame,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yet from my dugs he drew not this deceit.

speaker: BOY
text: Think you my uncle did dissemble, grandam?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ay, boy.

speaker: BOY
text: I cannot think it. Hark, what noise is this?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ah, who shall hinder me to wail and weep,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To chide my fortune and torment myself?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I’ll join with black despair against my soul

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And to myself become an enemy.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What means this scene of rude impatience?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To make an act of tragic violence.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Edward, my lord, thy son, our king, is dead.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Why grow the branches when the root is gone?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Why wither not the leaves that want their sap?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If you will live, lament. If die, be brief,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That our swift-wingèd souls may catch the King’s,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Or, like obedient subjects, follow him

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To his new kingdom of ne’er-changing night.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, so much interest have I in thy sorrow

speaker: DUCHESS
text: As I had title in thy noble husband.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I have bewept a worthy husband’s death

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And lived with looking on his images;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But now two mirrors of his princely semblance

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Are cracked in pieces by malignant death,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And I, for comfort, have but one false glass

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That grieves me when I see my shame in him.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou art a widow, yet thou art a mother,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And hast the comfort of thy children left,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But death hath snatched my husband from mine arms

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And plucked two crutches from my feeble hands,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Clarence and Edward. O, what cause have I,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thine being but a moiety of my moan,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To overgo thy woes and drown thy cries!

speaker: BOY
text: Ah, aunt, you wept not for our father’s death.

speaker: BOY
text: How can we aid you with our kindred tears?

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: Our fatherless distress was left unmoaned.

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: Your widow-dolor likewise be unwept!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Give me no help in lamentation.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I am not barren to bring forth complaints.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: All springs reduce their currents to mine eyes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That I, being governed by the watery moon,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: May send forth plenteous tears to drown the world.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ah, for my husband, for my dear lord Edward!

speaker: CHILDREN
text: Ah, for our father, for our dear lord Clarence!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Alas for both, both mine, Edward and Clarence!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What stay had I but Edward? And he’s gone.

speaker: CHILDREN
text: What stay had we but Clarence? And he’s gone.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What stays had I but they? And they are gone.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Was never widow had so dear a loss.

speaker: CHILDREN
text: Were never orphans had so dear a loss.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Was never mother had so dear a loss.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Alas, I am the mother of these griefs.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Their woes are parceled; mine is general.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: She for an Edward weeps, and so do I;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I for a Clarence weep; so doth not she.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: These babes for Clarence weep, and so do I;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I for an Edward weep; so do not they.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Alas, you three, on me, threefold distressed,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Pour all your tears. I am your sorrow’s nurse,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And I will pamper it with lamentation.

speaker: DORSET
text: Comfort, dear mother. God is much displeased

speaker: DORSET
text: That you take with unthankfulness His doing.

speaker: DORSET
text: In common worldly things, ’tis called ungrateful

speaker: DORSET
text: With dull unwillingness to repay a debt

speaker: DORSET
text: Which with a bounteous hand was kindly lent;

speaker: DORSET
text: Much more to be thus opposite with heaven,

speaker: DORSET
text: For it requires the royal debt it lent you.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Madam, bethink you, like a careful mother,

speaker: RIVERS
text: Of the young prince your son. Send straight for him.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Let him be crowned. In him your comfort lives.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Drown desperate sorrow in dead Edward’s grave

speaker: RIVERS
text: And plant your joys in living Edward’s throne.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sister, have comfort. All of us have cause

speaker: RICHARD
text: To wail the dimming of our shining star,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But none can help our harms by wailing them.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Madam my mother, I do cry you mercy;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I did not see your Grace. Humbly on my knee

speaker: RICHARD
text: I crave your blessing.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: God bless thee, and put meekness in thy breast,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Love, charity, obedience, and true duty.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Amen.
                 
                Aside
                .
               And make me die a good old man!

speaker: RICHARD
text: That is the butt end of a mother’s blessing;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I marvel that her Grace did leave it out.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You cloudy princes and heart-sorrowing peers

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That bear this heavy mutual load of moan,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now cheer each other in each other’s love.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Though we have spent our harvest of this king,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We are to reap the harvest of his son.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The broken rancor of your high-swoll’n hates,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But lately splintered, knit, and joined together,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Must gently be preserved, cherished, and kept.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Meseemeth good that with some little train

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Forthwith from Ludlow the young prince be fet

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Hither to London, to be crowned our king.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Why “with some little train,” my lord of Buckingham?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Marry, my lord, lest by a multitude

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The new-healed wound of malice should break out,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which would be so much the more dangerous

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By how much the estate is green and yet ungoverned.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Where every horse bears his commanding rein

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And may direct his course as please himself,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As well the fear of harm as harm apparent,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: In my opinion, ought to be prevented.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I hope the King made peace with all of us;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And the compact is firm and true in me.

speaker: RIVERS
text: And so in me, and so, I think, in all.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Yet since it is but green, it should be put

speaker: RIVERS
text: To no apparent likelihood of breach,

speaker: RIVERS
text: Which haply by much company might be urged.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Therefore I say with noble Buckingham

speaker: RIVERS
text: That it is meet so few should fetch the Prince.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And so say I.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then be it so, and go we to determine

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who they shall be that straight shall post to Ludlow.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Madam, and you, my sister, will you go

speaker: RICHARD
text: To give your censures in this business?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord, whoever journeys to the Prince,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For God’s sake let not us two stay at home.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For by the way I’ll sort occasion,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As index to the story we late talked of,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To part the Queen’s proud kindred from the Prince.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My other self, my council’s consistory,

speaker: RICHARD
text: My oracle, my prophet, my dear cousin,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I, as a child, will go by thy direction.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Toward Ludlow then, for we’ll not stay behind.
